response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong polyglot breadth (TypeScript/Node, DLang, some C/C++ and Java), but the resume shows no Kotlin, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, and no recent JVM or enterprise-Java work. The only IDE-adjacent experience is a brief 2014-2015 stint contributing C/C++ to NI LabWindows/CVI. The core required stack (Java + Kotlin + Spring + JVM internals) is essentially absent, making this a weak fit for an Enterprise IDE/JVM tooling role.
strengths: Brief prior experience contributing to an IDE product (NI LabWindows/CVI)
Long track record on complex, large-scale systems and developer tooling (MCP generators, CI/CD, test frameworks)
Strong software-craftsmanship habits: TDD, trunk-based development, open-source tooling authorship
concerns: No Kotlin experience and no demonstrated Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate work
Java appears only peripherally and over a decade ago; no evidence of deep JVM internals expertise
Recent work is TypeScript/Node and DLang focused, not aligned with enterprise-Java tooling

key_factors.factor: No demonstrated JVM internals or language-tooling depth
